ogTitle: Codrington vs Coghills Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Codrington and Coghills Creek.
heading: Codrington vs Coghills Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Codrington, Victoria and Coghills Creek, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Coghills Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Codrington (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Codrington or Coghills Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Codrington has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Coghills Creek scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Codrington scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Coghills Creek scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Codrington or Coghills Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Coghills Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
